title: gtp-25-1527
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Sg = 1/(1 - φ^2) * (1 - (Dhub/Dsw)^4) * tan(Θ); φ = Nvanes * Ivane / (2 * pi * Rhub * cos(Θ))
*   Sg = 2/3 * (1 - (Dhub/Dsw)^3) / (1 - (Dhub/Dsw)^2) * tan(Θ)

**2. Tables:**

*   **Table 1: Definition and parameter of several swirlers tested**

    | Tag       | Sg    | No. of vanes | Vane type | Vane angle (deg) | Effective area (m²) |
    | ---------- | ----- | ------------ | --------- | --------------- | ------------------- |
    | S0.7-S16  | 0.7   | 16           | Straight  | 43.25           | 6.7 x 10^-4         |
    | S1.1-C8   | 1.1   | 8            | Curved    | 45.18           | 8.8 x 10^-4         |
    | S1.1-C12  | 1.1   | 12           | Curved    | 35.90           | 7.8 x 10^-4         |
    | S1.1-S12  | 1.1   | 12           | Straight  | 55.95           | 7.8 x 10^-4         |
    | S1.1-S16  | 1.1   | 16           | Straight  | 55.95           | 6.7 x 10^-4         |

*   **Table 2: Experimental matrix**

    | Parameter                    | Value                 |
    | ---------------------------- | --------------------- |
    | Equivalence ratio (Φ)         | 1.05/1.10/1.13/1.15/1.18 |
    | Nozzle velocity (U0)         | 0.90 m/s               |
    | Geometric swirl (Sg)          | 0.7/1.1              |
    | Inlet temperature            | 298 K                     |
    | Inlet pressure               | 0.101 MPa                   |
    | Ammonia mole fraction (XNH3) | 1.0                   |

**3. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig. 2: DSLR flame images for test matrix of modular swirler geometries for various Φ**

    *   **Axes (Implied based on the image):**
        *   Y [mm]: Vertical axis showing flame height (0, 25.4, 50.8, 76.2, 101)
        *   R [mm]: Radial Axis showing flame width (-25.9, 0, 25.9)
    *   Labels:
        *   Titles for Rows : S1.1-C8, S1.1-C12, S1.1-S12, S0.7-S16, S1.1-S16
        *   Titles for Columns (approximate phi values) : phi = 1.05, 1.10, 1.13, 1.15, 1.18
        *   Label: U

title: gtp-25-1527
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text and image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**
NO_x decreases while N2O and NH3 increase as a function of phi.

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

**Figure 7 (top row):** NOx, N2O, and NH3 emissions  at 15% dry O2 corrected for Sg1.1-S16 at phi = 1.13

*   **Axes:**
    *   Y-axis: Distance from combustor wall (mm), labeled 0, 13, 25, 38, 51, 64, 76.
    *   X-axis: Distance from combustor center (mm), labeled 0, 13, 25.
*   **Curves:** Contours represent lines of constant concentration.
*   **Units:** Concentrations in dry ppm @ 15% O2
*   **Key values (Approximate, read from contours):**
    *   **NH3:** Contours range from approximately 56 to 250 dry ppm @ 15% O2
    *   **NOx** Contours 24ppm to 88ppm dry @ 15% 02
    *   **N2O** Contours ~2 to 6 ppm dry @ 15% O2

**Figure 7 (bottom row):** NOx, N2O, and NH3 emissions  at 15% dry O2 corrected for Sg1.1-C8 at phi = 1.13

*   **Axes:**
    *   Y-axis: Distance from combustor wall (mm), labeled 0, 13, 25, 38, 51, 64, 76.
    *   X-axis: Distance from combustor center (mm), labeled 0, 13, 25.
*   **Curves:** Contours represent lines of constant concentration.
*   **Units:** Concentrations in dry ppm @ 15% O2
*   **Key values (Approximate, read from contours):**
    *   **NH3:** Contours range from approximately 172 to 471 dry ppm @ 15% O2
    *   **NOx**  Contours 21ppm to 45ppm dry @ 15% 02
    *   **N2O**  Contours ~2 to 5 ppm dry @ 15% O2

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Distance downstream: 7.6 cm from flame
*   Residence Time: ~80 ms from the flame brush.
*   Equivalence Ratio (phi): 1.11, 1.13.
*   Swirler geometries: Sg1.1-C8, Sg1.1-C12, Sg1.1-S12, Sg1.1-S16, Sg0.7-S16.
*   O2 content in dry air = 15%
*   Vane Numbers: 16
